meta.title: Help center
meta.subtitle: Articles, guides and video tutorials for the platform

ui.changelogSubtitle: History of changes and new platform capabilities
ui.ctaTitle: social-media-v1 — autonomous AI agents for social networks
ui.ctaSubtitle: Connect a channel and get your first AI post in 15 minutes

articles.title: Quick start with social-media-v1
articles.summary: Full product overview: connect a channel, set up brand memory, launch agents and get your first post.

items: This video walks you through every step — from sign-up to your first published AI post. The whole process takes about 15 minutes.

articles.title: How to connect a Telegram channel
articles.summary: Step by step: grant the bot admin rights and link a channel so agents can prepare publications.

items: Connecting a channel is the first step after sign-up. It lets agents analyse past posts and publish new ones.

blocks.heading: Step 1. Create a brand
items: Open the «Brands» section in your account
Click «Create brand» and enter a name
Pick the default content language

blocks.heading: Step 2. Link the channel
items: Add the platform bot as an admin of your Telegram channel
Grant permission to post messages
Paste the channel link into the connection form and confirm

blocks.heading: What's next
items: Brand memory extracts tone of voice from recent posts
The content agent prepares drafts for approval
You choose the mode: «AI does it» or «AI proposes — you approve»

articles.title: How brand memory works
articles.summary: How AI extracts tone of voice, topics and stop words from your recent posts and keeps them between agent runs.

items: Brand memory is a structured representation of your style: tone of voice, key topics, forbidden phrasings and examples of strong posts.
It updates automatically when a channel is connected and on a manual rebuild.

blocks.heading: What's in the memory
items: Tone of voice: formality, emotion, sentence length
Topics: what the channel writes about most
Stop words and forbidden topics
Reference posts as a style sample

articles.title: AI agent operating modes
articles.summary: How «AI does it» differs from «AI proposes — you approve» and when to choose each mode.

items: Every brand has an operating mode that controls how autonomous the agents are.

blocks.heading: Available modes
items: AI does it — agents publish on schedule without you
AI proposes — you approve — every post needs confirmation
Paused — agents prepare drafts but publish nothing

articles.title: Content agent: overview
articles.summary: How the content agent plans topics, writes drafts and adapts them to the brand's tone of voice.

items: In this clip we look at how the content agent builds a content plan and prepares publications from brand memory.

articles.title: Two-factor authentication (2FA)
articles.summary: How to enable TOTP, save backup codes and what to do if you lose access to your authenticator app.

blocks.heading: Enabling 2FA
items: Open «Settings → Security»
Click «Enable 2FA» and scan the QR code in an authenticator app
Enter the six-digit code to confirm
Store the backup codes in a safe place

articles.title: Plans and billing
articles.summary: How the Solo, Pro and Network plans differ, how the trial works and how to switch plans.

blocks.heading: Plans
items: Solo — one brand and the core set of agents
Pro — multiple brands and extended analytics
Network — team roles and priority support
The trial requires no card. You can switch plans at any time in the billing section.

blocks.text: The trial is 7 days of full Pro access, free and with no card.

articles.title: Sign-up, email verification and first login
articles.summary: How to create an account, verify your email and sign in. What to do if the verification email doesn't arrive.

blocks.heading: Create an account
items: Open the sign-up page and enter your email and a password (12+ characters)
Receive an email with a six-digit code and enter it in the verification form
Accept the terms, privacy policy and user agreement
Sign in with your email and password — you'll land in the onboarding flow

blocks.heading: If the email doesn't arrive
items: Check the «Spam» and «Promotions» folders
Click «Resend code» — a new request is available once per 60 seconds
Make sure the email is spelled correctly
Contact support if the code still doesn't arrive after 5 minutes

blocks.text: Email verification is required — some features (channel connection, payments) won't work without it.

articles.title: What to do if a channel won't connect
articles.summary: The most common reasons Telegram channel connection fails and a step-by-step checklist of what to verify.

blocks.heading: Pre-connect checklist
items: The channel is public, or you have a direct invite link for the bot
The bot is added as an admin to the channel itself, not to a discussion chat
The bot has the «Post messages» and «Edit messages» permissions enabled
The channel link in the form is fully qualified (e.g. https://t.me/your_channel)

blocks.heading: If the channel still won't link
items: Remove the bot from the channel and add it back — Telegram may cache old permissions
Make sure you are the owner or an admin with the right to invite bots
Wait 30–60 seconds and click «Retry» in the connection form
If the error repeats, take a screenshot and send it to support

blocks.text: We use the official Telegram Bot API, so permissions inherit exactly what you granted in the channel — we cannot «expand» them on our side.

articles.title: All 8 AI agents: what each one does
articles.summary: A short overview of the MVP agent team: Content, Publisher, Analyst, Orchestrator, Brand Memory, Onboarding, Moderation, Notifications.

items: On MVP the team consists of eight agents — each covers one slice of channel work. They talk to each other through the orchestrator and a shared event bus, so they behave as a single organism rather than a set of scripts.

blocks.heading: Agent team
rows: Ideas, topics, post drafts in your tone of voice
Scheduling, publishing in Telegram, retries on failures
Tone of voice, topics, stop words, reference posts
Content checks for banned topics and risks

blocks.text: Media is a pipeline inside Content, not a separate agent. Strategist, Research, Engagement and others come post-MVP per the roadmap.

articles.title: Moderation agent: how we protect the channel
articles.summary: What the moderator checks before a post goes out, what content categories it blocks, and how to configure your own rules.

items: The moderator runs before publication: every post goes through a check for banned topics, toxicity and risks before it reaches Telegram. If anything looks off, the post is routed to manual approval — even in the «AI does it» mode.

blocks.heading: What is checked automatically
items: Banned topics from the brand memory
Stop words and phrasings you asked to avoid
Legally risky topics (medicine, finance, politics — depending on settings)
Tone-of-voice alignment

blocks.text: Trust > Speed is a product principle. Better to delay a post than ship something unacceptable.

articles.title: Publisher agent and publishing schedule
articles.summary: How the publisher queues posts, picks the publish time and what it does when Telegram's API returns errors.

items: Publisher makes sure a post goes out at the right time to the right channel. It accounts for the brand's timezone, Telegram Bot API limits and the priorities set by the orchestrator.

blocks.heading: What the publisher can do
items: Per-brand schedules by day and hour
Priority queue and rescheduling under load
Automatic retries on transient Telegram errors
Status logging: draft → scheduled → published → failed

blocks.text: All timestamps are stored in UTC, but the UI shows them in the timezone from your profile settings.

articles.title: Analyst agent: metrics and digests
articles.summary: Which metrics the analyst tracks, how to read the weekly digest and where to find per-post reports.

blocks.heading: What the analyst tracks
items: Reach, views and subscriber growth by day and week
Best and worst posts by engagement
Topics that resonate with your audience
Anomalies: spikes or drops in metrics
Once a week the analyst sends a digest: a metrics summary, the top 3 posts and recommendations on what to try next. Full reports live in the «Analytics» section of your account.

articles.title: When and how to rebuild brand memory
articles.summary: Why brand memory gets stale, how to trigger a manual rebuild and what changes in agent behaviour after an update.

items: Brand memory is built automatically when you connect a channel, but tone of voice and topics drift over time. A rebuild makes sense when the channel has clearly shifted direction or when you want to fold recent posts into the style sample.

blocks.heading: Trigger a rebuild
items: Open the «Brands» section and pick the brand
Click «Brand memory → Rebuild»
Confirm — the rebuild takes 2–5 minutes
Review the refreshed tone of voice and topic list on the same page

blocks.text: After a rebuild, the next Content agent run already uses the new style. Existing drafts can be regenerated manually.

articles.title: Stop words and banned topics
articles.summary: How to add words that must never appear in posts, and the topics moderation has to block.

blocks.heading: Add stop words
items: Open «Brands → Brand memory → Stop words»
Type the word or phrase and press Enter
Save — Content and Moderation pick up the rules immediately

blocks.heading: Good examples
items: Competitor names you don't want to mention
Slang and clichés that don't fit your tone of voice
Banned topics: financial advice, medicine, politics

blocks.text: Stop words are a soft rule (Content avoids them). Banned topics are a hard rule (Moderation blocks the post).

articles.title: Change your email and password
articles.summary: How to switch to a new email, change your password and what to do if you forgot it.

blocks.heading: Change email
items: Open «Settings → Account → Email»
Enter the new email and your current password
Confirm the code from the email sent to the new address

blocks.heading: Change password
items: Open «Settings → Account → Password»
Enter the current and new password (12+ characters)
Save — other active sessions will be signed out
If you forgot your password, click «Forgot password?» on the sign-in page, enter your email and receive a reset link. The link is valid for 1 hour.

articles.title: Manage active sessions
articles.summary: Where to see the list of devices you signed in from and how to terminate a session that isn't yours.

items: Each sign-in creates a session: we record the browser, operating system, country and last activity. That way you can spot a foreign login quickly.

blocks.heading: Terminate a session
items: Open «Settings → Sessions»
Find the suspicious or stale session
Click «Sign out» — the device will be logged out within 60 seconds
If in doubt, click «Sign out everywhere else» and change your password

articles.title: What to do if you lose access to 2FA
articles.summary: How to recover access to your account if the authenticator app is unavailable: backup codes and contacting support.

blocks.heading: Using backup codes
items: On the 2FA prompt click «Use a backup code»
Enter one of the one-time codes you saved
After signing in, immediately open «Settings → Security» and generate a fresh set of backup codes
If you've also lost your backup codes, contact support from the account's email. We'll request documents and details proving ownership — this can take up to 48 hours.

blocks.text: Each backup code works once. Print them or store them in a password manager right after enabling 2FA.

articles.title: Delete account and export data
articles.summary: How to export your data and delete the account. What happens to connected channels and the active subscription.

blocks.heading: Export data
items: Open «Settings → Account → Export»
Click «Request export» — we'll email you an archive within 24 hours
The archive contains your profile, brands, posts, brand memory and publication history

blocks.heading: Delete account
items: Open «Settings → Account → Delete account»
Confirm with your password and a 2FA code
The subscription is cancelled automatically, channel tokens are revoked
Data is purged after 30 days — during that window you can cancel the deletion

blocks.text: The platform bot stays in your Telegram channels. Remove it manually if you no longer need it there.

articles.title: Trial period and paying by card
articles.summary: How the 7-day Pro trial works, which cards are accepted and what happens after the trial ends.

blocks.heading: What the trial includes
items: 7 days of full access to Pro features
No card required — no charges happen
Up to 100 publications per brand during the 7 days
Full analytics, digests and brand memory
When the trial ends, the account moves to Solo with basic limits. Paid features are paused until you pick a plan.

blocks.text: We accept Visa, Mastercard, MIR and SBP cards. Companies can pay by invoice — see «Receipts and invoices for businesses».

articles.title: Switch plans and cancel the subscription
articles.summary: When upgrades and downgrades take effect, how to cancel and what happens to your data afterwards.

blocks.heading: Plan change logic
rows: Prorated charge for the rest of the period
At the start of the next billing period
Access stays until the paid period ends

blocks.heading: Cancel the subscription
items: Open «Settings → Plan and billing»
Click «Cancel subscription» and confirm
Features stay available until the period ends
After that the account moves to Solo with basic limits

blocks.text: Brand data, channels and brand memory are kept for 30 days after cancellation — during that window you can come back without losing anything.

articles.title: Limits, quotas and what happens when you exceed them
articles.summary: How many posts and channels each plan gives you, what the monthly per-brand cap means and how the platform notifies you when you go over.

blocks.heading: What happens when you exceed them
items: 60% of the limit — email and Telegram bot notification
80% — reminder plus a CTA to upgrade to Pro/Network
100% — auto-publishing is paused, agents keep preparing drafts
Reactivation — the next billing month or a plan upgrade

blocks.text: Going over the limit never charges you extra without consent — we don't do pay-per-use.

articles.title: Receipts and invoices for businesses
articles.summary: Where to download fiscal receipts, how to request an invoice for a legal entity and which details we need.

blocks.heading: Download a receipt
items: Open «Settings → Plan and billing → Payment history»
Click «Receipt» next to the payment you need
The receipt is issued under 54-FZ and suitable for accounting

blocks.heading: Invoice for a legal entity
items: Write to support: TIN, KPP, address, contact person
Within 1 business day we'll email an invoice
Once paid, the subscription activates automatically

blocks.text: Closing documents (acts, UPD) are issued on request — usually quarterly or at the end of the billing period.

articles.title: Brands: create, switch and delete
articles.summary: Why everything in the platform is organised around brands, how to create another one and how that differs from channels.

items: A brand is a container: its own brand memory, tone of voice and channels. One account can run several brands, switch between them and grant the team access to each one separately.

blocks.heading: Create a new brand
items: Open the brand switcher in the header
Click «Create brand» and enter a name
Pick the content language and timezone
Connect a channel to it and trigger the brand memory build

blocks.text: Brand count depends on the plan: Solo — 1, Pro — 3, Network — 10.

articles.title: Connect multiple channels to one brand
articles.summary: How to add a second and third channel to an existing brand and how agents split publications between them.

blocks.heading: Add another channel
items: Open «Brands → your brand → Channels»
Click «Add channel» and complete the connection flow
Pick a channel role: primary, experimental or mirror
Set a schedule for each channel separately

blocks.heading: How posts are split
items: Content prepares drafts at the brand level
Publisher distributes posts across channels per their schedules
Mirrors receive delayed copies of posts

blocks.text: Channel cap per brand: Solo — 1, Pro — 3, Network — 5.

articles.title: Disconnect a channel and revoke access
articles.summary: How to detach a channel from a brand, remove the bot from Telegram and what happens to accumulated memory and history.

blocks.heading: Disconnect a channel
items: Open «Brands → your brand → Channels»
Click «Disconnect» next to the channel
Confirm — publications stop immediately
Remove the bot from the Telegram channel manually if you don't need it any more

blocks.heading: What happens to data
items: Publication history stays in analytics
Brand memory is preserved — it belongs to the brand, not the channel
Posts scheduled for this channel are cancelled

articles.title: How we store your data and channel tokens
articles.summary: Where data physically lives, which tokens are encrypted, and who inside the company can reach it.

blocks.heading: Baseline principles
items: Data is encrypted in transit (TLS) and at rest (AES-256)
Channel tokens live in a hardened vault — engineers don't have direct access
Backups are encrypted and removed according to the retention policy
Logs do not store PII — we use anonymised events for debugging
A small group of engineers can reach production, every action is written to an audit log. Unless a user grants access to their brand, no one inside the company reads its content.

blocks.text: Full terms live in the privacy policy and user agreement. Both are linked from the footer of the site.

articles.title: What permissions the bot has in your channel
articles.summary: The minimal set of permissions the bot needs to work and why we don't ask for «full rights».

blocks.heading: What we need
items: Post messages — to publish content
Edit messages — to fix typos and refresh content
View channel stats — so the Analyst can compute metrics

blocks.heading: What we DON'T need
items: Delete other people's messages
Block or unblock subscribers
Change channel settings or invite admins

blocks.text: You can shrink the bot's permissions or remove it from the channel any time — we'll keep showing analytics for already-published posts.

articles.title: How to contact support
articles.summary: Channels you can reach us on, response times and what to attach to a ticket so we can solve it faster.

blocks.heading: How to reach us
items: Email: support@social.media — our main channel, we reply within 24 hours
Telegram bot @socialmedia_bot — short questions, replies during business hours
In-app chat — priority queue for Network plan customers

blocks.heading: What to attach to a ticket
items: Account email and brand name
Steps to reproduce the issue
Screenshots or a short screen recording
Timestamp of the failure (in your own timezone)

articles.title: Report a bug or suggest an idea
articles.summary: Where to file bug reports and feature requests, and how we decide what ships in the next release.

items: We build the product together with early users — every idea and bug report lands in a shared backlog and is prioritised transparently. The most requested items show up in the «Roadmap» section of this help center.

blocks.heading: Submit an idea
items: Open «Settings → Feedback»
Pick the type: «Idea» or «Bug»
Describe the goal, the desired outcome and steps to reproduce
Optionally «+1» other users' ideas — it helps with prioritisation

blocks.text: We respond to every idea: «picking up», «backlog» or «not a fit». No silent rejections.

changelog.title: New landing and design system
changelog.description: The public site has been fully refreshed: a new design system, a help center, and the «Changelog» and «Roadmap» sections.

changelog.title: Brands and channel connection
changelog.description: Added brands, Telegram channel connection and the first version of brand memory.

changelog.title: Auth, MFA and sessions
changelog.description: Sign-up, sign-in, email verification, two-factor authentication and session management.

items.title: 8 AI agents for Telegram
items.description: Content, publishing, analytics, orchestrator, onboarding, moderation, notifications and brand memory.

items.title: Research and Engagement
items.description: Agents for topic research and audience engagement.

items.title: Strategist and team roles
items.description: A strategy agent and roles for teams collaborating on brands.

items.title: Web and advanced analytics
items.description: Support for more platforms and deeper content performance analytics.
